Article.ArticleTitle: IDeate-Lung02: a Phase 3 study of second-line ifinatamab deruxtecan in patients with relapsed small cell lung cancer.

Abstract.AbstractText: Patients with small cell lung cancer (SCLC) have poor prognosis and limited treatment options beyond first-line therapy. B7 homolog 3 (B7-H3) is minimally expressed in normal tissues but highly expressed in SCLC. Ifinatamab deruxtecan (I-DXd), a B7-H3-directed antibody-drug conjugate, has demonstrated promising efficacy and a manageable safety profile in various tumours, including SCLC. IDeate-Lung02 is a global, randomized, open-label Phase 3 study of ~540 patients with relapsed SCLC. Adults with one prior line of platinum-based systemic therapy, ECOG performance status 0-1, and ≥1 measurable lesion (per RECIST 1.1) are eligible for study participation. Patients with asymptomatic untreated or previously treated brain metastases may participate. Patients are randomized 1:1 to receive I-DXd 12 mg/kg intravenously every 3 weeks or treatment of physician's choice (topotecan, amrubicin, or lurbinectedin). Dual primary endpoints are objective response rate (ORR) by blinded independent central review (BICR) and overall survival. Secondary endpoints include ORR by investigator; progression-free survival, duration of response, disease control rate, and time to response, all by BICR and investigator; patient-reported outcomes; and safety. IDeate-Lung02 will ascertain whether I-DXd treatment after only one prior line of systemic treatment improves outcomes for patients with relapsed SCLC compared with topotecan, amrubicin, or lurbinectedin.Clinical Trial Registration: NCT06203210.

MeshHeading.DescriptorName: Clinical Trials, Phase III as Topic

OtherAbstract.AbstractText: Small cell lung cancer (SCLC) is a fast-growing type of cancer, and most people are diagnosed after it has already spread. Many people respond to their first treatment, but the cancer often returns. When that happens, current treatments usually do not work well and can cause side effects. New treatment options are needed to help people live longer and feel better. The IDeate-Lung02 study is a global research study currently underway. It is testing a potential new treatment called ifinatamab deruxtecan (I-DXd) to see if it works better than standard chemotherapy. About 540 adults with SCLC that came back after one earlier treatment are taking part. I-DXd works by targeting a protein called B7-H3, which is found in most SCLC tumours and is linked to poor outcomes. I-DXd latches onto this protein and delivers cancer medicine directly to the tumour. This may help kill cancer cells while causing fewer side effects. Study participants are randomly placed in one of two groups: one group receives I-DXd, and the other receives typical chemotherapy. Researchers will compare how well each treatment works and how long people live after starting it. Earlier research suggests that I-DXd is safe and effective, even in people with SCLC whose cancer has spread to the brain. This study will help find out if I-DXd could become a new treatment option for people with SCLC whose cancer came back after one earlier treatment.

MedlineCitation.CoiStatement: Taofeek K. Owonikoko received consulting fees from AbbVie, Amgen, AstraZeneca, Bayer, BeiGene, BerGenBio, Boehringer Ingelheim, Bristol-Myers Squibb, Celgene, Coherus BioSciences, Daiichi Sankyo, Eisai, EMD Serono, Exelixis, G1 Therapeutics, Heat Biologics, Ipsen, Janssen, Jazz Pharmaceuticals, Lilly, MedImmune, Merck, Meryx Pharmaceuticals, Novartis, PharmaMar, Puma Biotechnology, Roche/Genentech, Takeda, Triptych Health Partners, and Xcovery; received travel support from AstraZeneca and Janssen; holds stock/ownership interests in Cambium Medical Technologies, Coherus Biosciences, GenCART, and Taobob LLC; and received research funding from AbbVie, Amgen, AstraZeneca/MedImmune, Bayer, Boehringer Ingelheim, Bristol-Myers Squibb, Calithera Biosciences, Corvus Pharmaceuticals, G1 Therapeutics, GlaxoSmithKline, Incyte, Loxi/Lilly, Merck, Meryx Pharmaceuticals, Novartis, Oncorus, Pfizer, Regeneron, and Roche/Genentech. Lauren Byers received consulting fees from MSD, AbbVie, Amgen, Arrowhead Pharmaceuticals, AstraZeneca, BeiGene, Chugai Pharma, Daiichi Sankyo, Genentech, Jazz Pharmaceuticals, and Puma Biotechnology; received honoraria from Clinical Care Options and UpToDate, and received research funding from Amgen, AstraZeneca, and Jazz Pharmaceuticals. Ying Cheng has no relationships to disclose. Hidetoshi Hayashi received consulting fees from AbbVie, AstraZeneca, Boehringer Ingelheim, Bristol-Myers Squibb, Daiichi Sankyo/UCB Japan, Janssen, and Novocure K.K.; received patent royalties from Sysmex; received honoraria from 3H Clinical Trial, Amgen, AstraZeneca Japan, Bristol-Myers Squibb Japan, Chugai Pharma, Daiichi Sankyo/UCB Japan, Guardant Health, Janssen, Lilly, Merck, MSD K.K. Tokyo Japan, Nippon Boehringer Ingelheim, Novartis, Ono Pharmaceutical, Pfizer, Takeda, and Sysmex; received research funding from A2 Healthcare, AbbVie, Ascent Development Services, Amgen, Astellas Pharma, AstraZeneca Japan, Bayer Yakuhin, Bristol-Myers Squibb, Chugai Pharma, CMIC CO., Ltd., Daiichi Sankyo, Eisai, EPS Holdings, GlaxoSmithKline K.K., IQVIA Services Japan K.K., Janssen, Kobayashi Pharmaceutical, Kyowa Kirin, Labcorp Drug Development, Lilly Japan, Medpace Japan K.K., Merck, MSD, Nippon Boehringer Ingelheim, Nippon Kayaku, Novartis, Otsuka, Pfizer, PRA Health Sciences, Shionogi, Sanofi, SRL Medisearch Inc., Syneos Health, Taiho Pharmaceutical, and Takeda. Luis G. Paz-Ares received consulting fees from AbbVie, Amgen, AstraZeneca, Bayer, Bristol-Myers Squibb, GlaxoSmithKline, Janssen, Lilly, Merck, Mirati Therapeutics, MSD, Novartis, PharmaMar, Pfizer, Regeneron, Roche, Sanofi, and Takeda; holds leadership positions with Altum Sequencing and Stab Therapeutics; is a member of the speakers’ bureaus for AstraZeneca, Bristol-Myers Squibb, Lilly, Merck Serono, MSD Oncology, Pfizer, and Roche/Genentech; holds stock/ownership interests in Altum Sequencing and Stab Therapeutics; and received honoraria from Amgen, AstraZeneca, Bayer, BeiGene, Boehringer Ingelheim, Bristol-Myers Squibb, Daiichi Sankyo, GlaxoSmithKline, Janssen, Lilly, Medscape, Merck Serono, Mirati Therapeutics, MSD, Novartis, PharmaMar, Pfizer, Regeneron, Roche/Genentech, Sanofi, and Takeda. Haichuan Hu is an employee of MSD. Mei Teng, Meng Qian, and Juliette Godard are employees of Daiichi Sankyo, Inc. Cecilio Roi Garcia is an employee of Daiichi Sankyo, Inc. and holds stock/ownership interests in ADC Therapeutics. Charles M. Rudin serves on advisory boards for Auron, Bridge Medicines, DISCO, Earli, and Harpoon Pharmaceuticals; and holds stock/ownership interests in Auron, DISCO, and Earli. The authors have no other relevant affiliations or financial involvement with any organization or entity with a financial interest in or financial conflict with the subject matter or materials discussed in the manuscript apart from those disclosed.
